original_hypothesis.title: Missing CrocSwap Token Approval Prevents All Swaps
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The _swapOFTForNote function calls CrocSwap.swap to exchange asdUSDC for NOTE, but never approves CrocSwap to spend the router's asdUSDC tokens. With reserveFlags=0, CrocSwap uses transferFrom to pull tokens, which requires prior ERC20 approval. The router has no function to set this approval, and the deployment script doesn't set it. Every swap call fails.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Combined with TECH-H004 (inverted swap direction), this creates a second independent reason all swaps fail. Even if the swap direction were fixed, the missing approval would still prevent swaps. The router contract has no external function to set approvals.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: deployASDRouter.js contains no approval call for CrocSwap; ASDRouter has no function to set external approvals
preconditions: No external mechanism sets the approval

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: No IERC20.approve call for CrocSwap anywhere in contract; no admin function to set arbitrary approvals
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: DEX swap requires token approval; router has no way to grant it
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: IERC20(asdUSDC).approve(crocSwapAddress, type(uint256).max) in constructor or before swap

analysis_questions: Does CrocSwap support a non-transferFrom token intake with reserveFlags != 0?
Can the owner add an arbitrary approval setter function post-deployment?
Is there any way to set approval without a contract function?
validation_requirements: Verify CrocSwap's token intake mechanism for reserveFlags=0
Confirm no approval is set in deployment or post-deployment scripts
Test swap call failure mode with zero approval

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Not an attack; a code defect causing total protocol non-functionality. Every swap returns (false, data), triggering refund path. Users always receive asdUSDC instead of ASD.
prerequisites: CrocSwap requires transferFrom for token intake

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Protocol completely non-functional for its primary use case
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires contract redeployment with approval logic

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No integration test with CrocSwap requiring approval

validation_result.mechanism: missing ERC20 approval for DEX swap
validation_result.impact_summary: The ASDRouter never approves the CrocSwap DEX to spend its asdUSDC tokens. With reserveFlags=0, CrocSwap uses transferFrom which requires approval, so every swap call fails, rendering the core protocol functionality non-operational.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The _swapOFTForNote function at asdRouter.sol line ~193 calls crocSwapAddress.call with ICrocSwapDex.swap.selector, passing reserveFlags=0 (the last parameter). With reserveFlags=0, CrocSwap uses standard ERC20 transferFrom to pull tokens from the caller (the router). However, there is no IERC20(asdUSDC).approve(crocSwapAddress, ...) call anywhere in the entire ASDRouter contract. The constructor does not set an approval. There is no admin function to set approvals. The deployment script (deployASDRouter.js) does not set any approval post-deployment. Since CrocSwap cannot transferFrom without approval, the swap always fails. The low-level call pattern means it returns (false, ...) rather than reverting, triggering the refund path where users receive asdUSDC instead of ASD tokens.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Exhaustive search of asdRouter.sol reveals exactly two approve calls: (1) IERC20(_from).approve(asdUSDC, amountLD) at lzCompose - this approves the asdUSDC contract, not CrocSwap; (2) IERC20(noteAddress).approve(_asdVault, _amountNote) at _depositNoteToASDVault - this approves the ASD vault for NOTE, not CrocSwap for asdUSDC. No other approve calls exist. The CrocSwap DEX interface specifies reserveFlags as the last parameter of swap(), and it is set to 0, which means standard ERC20 transferFrom is used.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The swap call passes reserveFlags=0 (last parameter), meaning CrocSwap will use transferFrom to pull asdUSDC from the router. However, no IERC20(asdUSDC).approve(crocSwapAddress, amount) call exists anywhere in the contract before this swap call. The transferFrom will always fail due to zero allowance, causing successSwap to be false and every swap to fail.

The approve call here is for the asdUSDC contract to spend the OFT tokens (_from), NOT for CrocSwap to spend asdUSDC. After the deposit succeeds and the router holds asdUSDC, _swapOFTForNote is called but CrocSwap has no approval to pull the asdUSDC.

calling_sequence: 1. lzCompose receives OFT tokens and decodes composeMsg
2. IERC20(_from).approve(asdUSDC, amountLD) - approves asdUSDC contract for OFT tokens
3. ASDUSDC(asdUSDC).deposit(_from, amountLD) - deposits OFT, router receives asdUSDC
6. CrocSwap.swap internally calls transferFrom(router, crocSwap, amount) for asdUSDC
10. lzCompose enters refund path, sends asdUSDC to _cantoRefundAddress
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The critical missing code is an approve call such as IERC20(asdUSDC).approve(crocSwapAddress, type(uint256).max) either in the constructor or before the swap call in _swapOFTForNote. Without it, CrocSwap (at reserveFlags=0) cannot pull tokens via transferFrom, and every swap fails. The contract has no admin function, fallback mechanism, or deployment script step that could set this approval.

deployment_assumptions_checked: No approval set in deployment script for CrocSwap
No admin function exists to set arbitrary approvals post-deployment
config_validation.deployment_evidence: deployASDRouter.js deploys the contract and only calls updateWhitelist. No approve calls are made. The contract constructor sets addresses but does not call any approve. There is no owner-only or public function to set ERC20 approvals for CrocSwap.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an attack but a code defect causing total protocol non-functionality. Every lzCompose call that reaches the swap step will fail. The flow is: (1) User sends OFT stablecoin cross-chain via LayerZero, (2) ASDRouter receives tokens and deposits to asdUSDC successfully, (3) Router attempts to swap asdUSDC for NOTE via CrocSwap, (4) CrocSwap's transferFrom fails due to zero allowance, (5) Low-level call returns false, (6) User is refunded in asdUSDC instead of receiving ASD. Users consistently receive asdUSDC (a possibly illiquid/unfamiliar token) instead of the ASD they expected.
validation_result.impact_assessment: CRITICAL - The primary protocol function (cross-chain stablecoin to ASD conversion) is completely non-functional. All users who send OFT stablecoins expecting ASD will receive asdUSDC refunds instead. The protocol cannot fulfill its core use case without contract redeployment or upgrade (which is not supported by the current non-upgradeable architecture).
recommended_mitigations: Add an infinite approval for CrocSwap in the constructor: IERC20(asdUSDC).approve(crocSwapAddress, type(uint256).max)
Alternatively, add a per-swap approval in _swapOFTForNote before the swap call: IERC20(_oftAddress).approve(crocSwapAddress, _amount)

Add an owner-only function to set arbitrary ERC20 approvals as a safety valve: function setApproval(address token, address spender, uint256 amount) external onlyOwner { IERC20(token).approve(spender, amount); }
Contract must be redeployed with the fix since no upgrade mechanism exists
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy ASDUSDC, a mock CrocSwap, and ASDRouter on a testnet. 2. Whitelist a test OFT in ASDUSDC. 3. Send OFT tokens to the router. 4. Call lzCompose with valid compose message. 5. Observe that the swap step fails (successSwap = false) and user receives asdUSDC refund. 6. Verify IERC20(asdUSDC).allowance(router, crocSwap) == 0.
uncertainties: CrocSwap's exact behavior with reserveFlags=0 is based on documented Ambient DEX behavior - the source code for CrocSwap is not in scope, but the standard expectation for reserveFlags=0 is transferFrom-based token intake
There could theoretically be an external operational script not provided that sets the approval, but the deployment script provided does not do this
additional_testing_needed: Verify CrocSwap's actual token intake behavior with reserveFlags=0 on Canto mainnet
Check if there are any additional deployment/setup scripts not provided that might set approvals
Integration test with actual CrocSwap contract to confirm the swap fails due to missing approval
